user_prompt: explain bernoulli’s principle using an airplane wing and airflow visualization

style_bible.visual_style: Clean, scientific, and realistic with clear airflow visualization using colored smoke or digital flow lines; focus on clarity and educational aesthetics.
style_bible.camera_rules: Use smooth, continuous camera movements with no jump cuts; maintain consistent screen direction; vary framing from wide to close-up to emphasize details.
style_bible.lighting_rules: Soft, diffuse lighting from above and side to highlight wing contours and airflow; neutral color temperature to maintain clarity and realism.
style_bible.continuity_rules: Keep the airplane wing as the primary subject centered in frame; maintain the same hangar or wind tunnel background with consistent lighting and airflow effects across scenes.

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Introduce the airplane wing in a wind tunnel with visible airflow starting to move.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY:
- primary subject: airplane wing, metallic surface, medium size, fixed horizontally
- environment: indoor wind tunnel, gray walls, measurement instruments visible

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: wide shot, wing centered horizontally
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly from left to right
- subject & action: airflow begins as faint smoke lines approaching the wing by 1s, smoke thickens by 2s, flow smoothly wraps around leading edge by 3s
- environment details: wind tunnel fans visible, measurement gauges on walls, smooth concrete floor
- lighting: soft overhead light, neutral white, minimal shadows
- cinematic look: clean digital clarity, moderate contrast, deep focus

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: medium shot, wing leading edge and upper surface
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow pan right to left
- subject & action: airflow smoke intensifies, lines separate over and under wing, visible pressure difference begins
- environment details: control panel with blinking lights, airflow sensors
- lighting: side fill light from left, soft shadows
- cinematic look: crisp detail, natural colors, shallow depth of field on wing surface

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: close-up on wing upper surface
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: static
- subject & action: smoke lines accelerate over upper wing surface, smooth laminar flow visible
- environment details: textured wing surface, small rivets, slight reflections
- lighting: directional soft light from top left
- cinematic look: high detail, subtle film grain for texture

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.camera: slow dolly left to right, then slow pan right to left, ending static close-up
scenes.audio_cue: soft ambient wind tunnel hum with gentle airflow whoosh

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Show airflow splitting and pressure differences causing lift around the wing.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY:
- primary subject: airplane wing, metallic surface, medium size, fixed horizontally
- environment: indoor wind tunnel, gray walls, measurement instruments visible

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: medium shot, wing profile from side
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow tilt upward
- subject & action: airflow smoke splits clearly above and below wing by 1s, upper flow speeds up by 2s, lower flow slower and denser by 3s
- environment details: digital pressure readouts on side wall, airflow sensors
- lighting: soft directional light from upper right
- cinematic look: crisp focus, moderate contrast, natural color

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: close-up on wing underside
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: slow pan left to right
- subject & action: denser airflow under wing visible, slight turbulence near trailing edge by 5s
- environment details: small airflow vortices visualized by smoke curls, sensor lights blinking
- lighting: side fill light from left, soft shadows
- cinematic look: high detail, slight film grain

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: medium wide shot, wing and airflow from front angle
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly backward
- subject & action: airflow lines show pressure difference, wing generating visible lift force by 9s, wing slightly rising
- environment details: wind tunnel structure, measurement instruments
- lighting: balanced soft light from above
- cinematic look: clear, educational style, deep focus

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.camera: slow tilt up, then slow pan left to right, ending with slow dolly backward
scenes.audio_cue: increasing wind tunnel airflow with subtle mechanical hum

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Demonstrate the Bernoulli effect with accelerated airflow over wing and resulting lift.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY:
- primary subject: airplane wing, metallic surface, medium size, fixed horizontally
- environment: indoor wind tunnel, gray walls, measurement instruments visible

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: close-up on upper wing surface
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: slow pan right to left
- subject & action: airflow smoke accelerates over upper surface, thinning and stretching by 2s, wing surface shows slight flex by 3s
- environment details: smooth wing texture, subtle reflections
- lighting: soft directional light from top left
- cinematic look: high detail, natural colors, shallow depth of field

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: medium shot, wing from side with airflow
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow dolly forward
- subject & action: airflow lines clearly faster above wing, slower below; wing visibly lifting by 6s
- environment details: pressure gauges showing differential, airflow sensors
- lighting: balanced soft light
- cinematic look: clean, educational clarity

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: wide shot, full wing and wind tunnel
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow crane up and back
- subject & action: wing held stable in airflow, smoke lines smoothly flowing, clear demonstration of lift force
- environment details: wind tunnel structure, control panels
- lighting: soft overhead light, neutral tone
- cinematic look: crisp, final educational frame

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.camera: slow pan right to left, then slow dolly forward, finishing with slow crane up and back
scenes.audio_cue: steady wind tunnel airflow with clear whooshing sounds
